title: Axiom
notes: Axiom is a meta-mathematical/philosophical notion about formal systems; Mathlib uses Lean's built-in `axiom` keyword rather than formalizing 'axiom' as a declaration.

title: Abacus
notes: The abacus is a physical calculating tool, not a mathematical concept formalized in Mathlib.

title: Algorithm
notes: The general concept of 'algorithm' is a meta-mathematical/informal notion; Mathlib formalizes specific computable functions and decidability but not 'algorithm' as a standalone concept.

title: Cryptography
notes: Cryptography as a discipline is not formalized in Mathlib; only adjacent number-theoretic primitives (e.g., modular arithmetic, finite fields) exist, none of which formalize cryptography itself.

title: Classical mechanics
notes: Classical mechanics is a physics theory; Mathlib formalizes prerequisite mathematics (ODEs, symplectic forms, calculus of variations components) but not the physical theory itself.

notes: The general notion of 'mathematical proof' is a meta-level concept; in Lean/Mathlib, proofs are terms of propositions (Prop), not a named declaration to point to.

title: Combinatorics

notes: Mathlib has computability and many-one reductions (Mathlib.Computability.Reduce) but no formalization of polynomial-time reductions, the class NP, or NP-hardness.

title: Method of analytic tableaux
notes: Mathlib's only 'tableau' occurrences refer to Young tableaux (combinatorics) and simplex-algorithm tableaux; the analytic/semantic tableau proof procedure is not formalized.

notes: No declarations for cographs (P4-free graphs) found in Mathlib; SimpleGraph theory is present but cographs specifically are not formalized.

title: Paraconsistent logic
notes: Mathlib's logic is classical and includes the principle of explosion (absurd); paraconsistent logic as a non-classical system is not formalized in Mathlib.

title: Determinacy
notes: No declarations relating to determinacy, Gale–Stewart games, winning strategies in infinite games, or Borel determinacy were found in Mathlib.

title: Informal logic
notes: Informal logic concerns non-formal standards for analyzing natural-language argumentation, which is outside the scope of formal mathematical libraries.

title: Order of operations
notes: Order of operations is a notational/parsing convention, not a mathematical object; Mathlib relies on Lean's operator precedence at the parser level rather than formalizing the convention itself.

title: Coefficient of determination
notes: Mathlib has no statistics/regression formalization; no declarations for R², explained variance, or sample correlation were found.

title: 3-manifold
notes: Mathlib has a general manifold framework (ChartedSpace, IsManifold) but no declarations specific to 3-manifolds or dimension-3 topology (no geometrization, Heegaard splittings, etc.).

title: Angular velocity
notes: Angular velocity is a physics/kinematics concept; Mathlib does not formalize it (no AngularVelocity declarations found).

title: Circles of Apollonius
notes: Mathlib contains `dist_sq_add_dist_sq_eq_two_mul_dist_midpoint_sq_add_half_dist_sq` (Apollonius's Theorem, a median-length identity), but no formalization of the Circles of Apollonius themselves (locus of constant distance ratio, the two orthogonal families, the triangle's Apollonius circles, Apollonius's tangency problem, or the Apollonian gasket).

title: Numeral prefix
notes: Numeral prefixes are a linguistic/etymological topic, not a mathematical concept formalizable in Mathlib.

mathlib_decls.evidence: Mathlib/Probability/Kernel/IonescuTulcea/Traj.lean:518 — `noncomputable def traj (a : ℕ) : Kernel (Π i : Iic a, X i) (Π n, X n)` builds the distribution of the infinite trajectory by iterating transition kernels (Ionescu–Tulcea)

Mathlib/Probability/Kernel/Invariance.lean:39 — `def Invariant (κ : Kernel α α) (μ : Measure α) : Prop := μ.bind κ = μ` (stationary distribution)
